reviews.review_msg: Failed out of this class because he taught me nothing and I dreaded math soso much��
amazing ! Exams are super fair and he is very funny! Highly recommend
GREAT PROF! Super fair Would highly recommend for math 154 students! Watch jamie mulhollands videos if you dont understand his accent! It's the same thing.
his exams are super easy and basic. I loved his class, he is a very funny guy.

I did not like him at first. However, as the semester progressed I began really enjoying his lectures! He is funny and makes waking up for an 8:00 am lecture worth while. His tests stay true to what he teaches in class so as long as you understand the examples you should do great! Final exam was very fair,

His accent is somewhat thick, but by all means understandable. He certainly knows his stuff, and can convey his message well. However, the way he explained differential equations was somewhat confusing, so go use Jamie Mulholland's. He recommends it. What you should be careful of is Petra Menz and her minions' Inconsistent marking on the IQs. (HW)

Baris is a very nice guy! I love him. This class wasn't that hard. Make sure to practice because practice makes perfect :) !!!!!!! His accent is very thicccc but hes chill. His exam was fairly easy. The format of the his exams is exactly like his exams. love my boy baris!

Baris is a nice guy and a good prof. His accent is bit strong and he doesn't like turning up the microphone, so it might be a little hard to understand at the beginning. But you'll get used to it. He explains the concepts very well so be sure to go to the lecture even though it's not mandatory, and he also gives exam tips during the lecture.

Baris is a hard prof to understand with his accent, but throughout the semester you will get used to it. If you don't understand anything watch Jamie Mulholland on Youtube. The thing I really like about Baris were his exams. He uses VERY similar exams to the practice ones and also told us a question that would be on the finals. Super Fair.

The whole semester I wanted to like Baris. He was a really nice guy and he genuinely wanted to see his students succeed. However, the mixture of his accent and his mic constantly being set too low (despite students constantly asking him to turn it up) made his lectures hard to understand. I had to end up teaching myself most of the course.

He's a respected prof and always willing to answer questions after lectures. His lecture is interesting (personally recommended to attend his lectures since his notes are quite helpful; if you have any questions about IQ or OQ, go to the ACW). The course content would be easy if you had taken AP Calc in high school.

Amazing person and an even better prof. Takes time to explain to core concepts of eat topic and then makes you apply it. If you can do the homeworks then youll succeed as he does not try to trick you, just test your learning. Very helpful and is overall a nice guy to talk to.

Very thick accent often with microphone too low, writing is chicken scratch, screened is zoomed out too far despite constant complaints from students during lectures, notes are not always clear or relevant to understand concepts. A combination of all of this left me spending far too much time trying to teach myself, falling behind in other classes

He's funny, makes morning classes bearable. To prepare for all exams, I did practice test from the past year AND looked at the IQ, that's it (less than 2 hour to prepare). I ended up with an A for all midterms and final (and in class). If you don't understand him, watch Jamie Mulholland on youtube(same thing). Easy to get an A b/c he is SUPER fair.

He seemed like a nice guy but the course itself was a bit tragic. His accent is really heavy and his way of teaching isn't that efficient anyway. His notes aren't written by him, and neither are his assignments. He just uses Jamie Mullholland's stuff 90% of the time. The only good thing about him I think is that he smiles all the time.
Awesome guy, super fair.

Tough to understand and lectures could be pretty unclear (not just due to accent). Nice guy but for first year calc it was rough to have to fight to understand his words, let alone understanding some tough concepts beyond that. He was funny tho, made the 830 lecture bearable.

His accent was hard to understand in the beginning, but he's good with explanations. Also, his midterms were very easy if you just practiced a little bit "This is hard, but it's also easy" - Baris 2k18
He has a heavy accent and takes short cuts when doing examples, but his exams are a lot easier than assignments and examples he does during lecture.
Hard to understand but great lectures
Go to lectures and practice as much as you can.
He was great 10/10
